External Data

Libraries

# libraries
library(rvest)
library(tidyverse)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(sf)
library(gt)
library(httr)
library(tidyjson)
library(jsonlite)
library(glue)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)

# measles_year dataset
measles_year <- read_csv(here::here("data", "measles_year.csv"))
head(measles_year)
# A tibble: 6 × 15
  Region Country `Country Code`  Year `Total Pop.` `Annualized Pop. 2025`
  <chr>  <chr>   <chr>          <dbl>        <dbl>                  <dbl>
1 AFRO   Algeria DZA             2013     38414172               38414172
2 AFRO   Algeria DZA             2014     39205031               39205031
3 AFRO   Algeria DZA             2015     40019529               40019529
4 AFRO   Algeria DZA             2016     40850721               40850721
5 AFRO   Algeria DZA             2017     41689299               41689299
6 AFRO   Algeria DZA             2018     42505035               42505035
# ℹ 9 more variables: `Total Suspected Cases` <dbl>, `Total Measles` <dbl>,
#   `Total Lab Measles` <dbl>, `Total Epi Measles` <dbl>,
#   `Total Clinical Measles` <dbl>, `Total Measles per Million` <dbl>,
#   `Total Discarded` <dbl>, `Total Discarded per Million` <dbl>, ASCII <chr>

Motivation:

Measles continues to affect millions of people worldwide each year, despite major scientific advancements and the availability of an effective vaccine. We aim to explore the socioeconomic and geographic factors that may influence measles infection rates. By analyzing these patterns, we hope to better understand disparities across regions and identify populations at higher risk. Countries in Africa are of particular interest, as they are among the most heavily impacted by the measles disease.

Source: https://www.who.int/news/item/05-12-2019-more-than-140-000-die-from-measles-as-cases-surge-worldwide

Webscraping:

Like many diseases, measles transmission is influenced by environmental conditions. Research suggests that the virus is less likely to thrive in extremely cold or hot temperatures, while lower relative humidity can make it easier to spread. It is also important to consider a country’s life expectancy at birth, as it offers insight into overall healthcare quality and the level of development.

Source: https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.4161/hv.27826#d1e168

get_info_from_page():

pulls information from a website.

get_info_from_page <- function(page, css_selector, node, type) {
  
  # grab the css and node elements from the page
  if (type == "temp"){
  page |>
    html_element(css_selector) |>
    html_elements(node) |>
    html_text(trim = TRUE)}
  
  else if(type == "url"){
  page |>
    html_elements(node) |> 
    html_attr("href")}
  
  else {
  page |>
    html_elements(node) |> 
    html_text2()}}

Scrape Average Yearly Temperature

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_countries_by_average_yearly_temperature

Data Description: Wikipedia has data on the average yearly temperature across multiple countries.

scrape_temp_page():

calls get_info_from_page() and grabs different elements, combining into clean dataframe.

scrape_temp_page <- function(url) {
    
    # 1 second crawl delay
    Sys.sleep(1)
    
    # read in page
    page <- read_html(url)
    
    # country/region
    country_region <- get_info_from_page(page, 
                                         css_selector = "table.wikitable", 
                                         node = "tbody tr td:nth-child(2)",
                                         type = "temp")
      
    # continent
    continent <- get_info_from_page(page,
                                    css_selector = "table.wikitable", 
                                    node = "tbody tr td:nth-child(3)",
                                    type = "temp")
    
    # temp 
    temp <- get_info_from_page(page, 
                               css_selector = "table.wikitable", 
                               node = "tbody tr td:nth-child(4)",
                               type = "temp")
    
    # Put page elements into a data frame / tibble
    tibble(Country = country_region,
           Continent = continent,
           Temperature = temp)}
# apply to temp page
url <- "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_countries_by_average_yearly_temperature"
temperature_df <- scrape_temp_page(url)

clean temperature dataframe:

# split temperature col into celsius and fahrenheit
temperature_df <- temperature_df |>
  mutate(`Avg. Celsius` = as.numeric(str_extract(Temperature, "\\d+\\.\\d+")),
         `Avg. Fahrenheit` = as.numeric(str_extract(Temperature, "(?<=\\()\\d+\\.\\d+"))) |>
  select(-Temperature)

head(temperature_df)
# A tibble: 6 × 4
  Country      Continent     `Avg. Celsius` `Avg. Fahrenheit`
  <chr>        <chr>                  <dbl>             <dbl>
1 Burkina Faso Africa                  30.4              86.7
2 Mali         Africa                  29.2              84.6
3 Aruba        South America           29.2              84.5
4 Senegal      Africa                  28.9              84.0
5 Mauritania   Africa                  28.8              83.9
6 Tokelau      Oceania                 28.7              83.7

Scrape Life Expectancy

Source: https://data.worldbank.org/country

Data Description: The world bank group has large amounts of data relating to a given country’s socioeconomic factors.

grab_urls():

scrapes the data.worldbank.org page for all of the urls for each country

grab_urls <- function(country_url){
  # read page
  page <- read_html(country_url)
    
  # links
  country_links <- get_info_from_page(page, 
                                      css_selector = "", 
                                      node = "section.nav-item ul li a",
                                      type = "url")
  
  # country
  country <- get_info_from_page(page, 
                                css_selector = "", 
                                node = "section.nav-item ul li a",
                                type = "text")
  # full url
  tibble(link = paste0("https://data.worldbank.org", country_links),
         country = country)}

Keep relevant countries for more efficient webscraping

# grab urls
country_url <- "https://data.worldbank.org/country"
all_country_urls <- grab_urls(country_url)

# countries are not exactly named the same
measles_year |>
  filter(!ASCII %in% all_country_urls$country) |>
  pull(Country) |>
  unique()
 [1] "Congo"                                               
 [2] "Democratic Republic of the Congo"                    
 [3] "Gambia"                                              
 [4] "United Republic of Tanzania"                         
 [5] "Bahamas"                                             
 [6] "Bolivia (Plurinational State of)"                    
 [7] "Saint Kitts and Nevis"                               
 [8] "Saint Lucia"                                         
 [9] "Saint Vincent and the Grenadines"                    
[10] "United States of America"                            
[11] "Venezuela (Bolivarian Republic of)"                  
[12] "Egypt"                                               
[13] "Iran (Islamic Republic of)"                          
[14] "Somalia"                                             
[15] "Yemen"                                               
[16] NA                                                    
[17] "Kyrgyzstan"                                          
[18] "Netherlands (Kingdom of the)"                        
[19] "Republic of Moldova"                                 
[20] "Slovakia"                                            
[21] "United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland"
[22] "Democratic People's Republic of Korea"               
[23] "Cook Islands"                                        
[24] "Lao People's Democratic Republic"                    
[25] "Micronesia (Federated States of)"                    
[26] "Niue"                                                
[27] "Republic of Korea"                                   
# clean country col to match
all_country_urls <- all_country_urls |>
  separate_wider_delim(country, 
                       delim = ",", 
                       names = c("country", "country_extra"), 
                       too_few = "align_start") |>
  mutate(country = str_replace_all(country, "(?i)st\\.", "Saint"),
         country = case_when(str_detect(country_extra, "Dem\\.") ~ paste("Democratic", country),
                             TRUE ~ country),
         country = recode_values(country,
                                 "Lao PDR" ~ "Lao",
                                 "Kyrgyz Republic" ~ "Kyrgyzstan",
                                 "Slovak Republic" ~ "Slovakia",
                                 default = country)) |>
         select(-country_extra)


# clean ASCII col to match
measles_year <- measles_year |>
  mutate(ASCII = str_replace_all(ASCII, "\\s*\\(.*?\\)", ""),
         ASCII = str_remove_all(ASCII, "(?i)\\bRepublic of (the )?"),
         ASCII = str_remove_all(ASCII, "(?i)\\bPeople's\\s*"),
         ASCII = recode_values(ASCII,
                          "United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland" ~ "Ireland",
                          "United States of America" ~ "United States",
                          "Lao Democratic Republic" ~ "Lao",
                          "United Tanzania" ~ "Tanzania",
                          "Democratic Republic of the Congo" ~ "Democratic Congo",
                          default = ASCII),
         ASCII = str_trim(ASCII))

# check changes were made
measles_year |>
  filter(!ASCII %in% all_country_urls$country) |>
  pull(Country) |>
  unique()
[1] NA             "Cook Islands" "Niue"

The website does not like large calls, so we have to resort to batches.

# grab africa countries 
measles_africa <- measles_year |>
  filter(Region == "AFRO")

# keep only countries in measles_year 
africa_country_urls <- all_country_urls |>
  filter(country %in% measles_africa$ASCII)

# grab amro countries
measles_amro <- measles_year |>
  filter(Region == "AMRO")

amro_country_urls <- all_country_urls |>
  filter(country %in% measles_amro$ASCII)

# grab emro countries
measles_emro <- measles_year |>
  filter(Region == "EMRO")

emro_country_urls <- all_country_urls |>
  filter(country %in% measles_emro$ASCII)

# grab euro countries
measles_euro <- measles_year |>
  filter(Region == "EURO")

euro_country_urls <- all_country_urls |>
  filter(country %in% measles_euro$ASCII)

# grab searo countries
measles_searo <- measles_year |>
  filter(Region == "SEARO")

searo_country_urls <- all_country_urls |>
  filter(country %in% measles_searo$ASCII)

# grab wpro countries
measles_wpro <- measles_year |>
  filter(Region == "WPRO")

wpro_country_urls <- all_country_urls |>
  filter(country %in% measles_wpro$ASCII)

scrape_country_page():

scrapes a country’s data.worldbank.org page for its life expectancy

scrape_country_page <- function(url) {
    
    # 1 second crawl delay
    Sys.sleep(runif(1, min = 4, max = 6))
    
    # read in page
    page <- read_html(url)
    
    # le_node
    le_node <- page |> 
    html_elements(".indicator-item") |> 
    keep(~str_detect(html_text(.x), "Life expectancy at birth"))
      
    # life_exp
    life_exp <- le_node |> 
    html_element(".indicator-item__data-info span") |> 
    html_text2() |> 
    as.numeric()
    
    # year 
    year <- le_node |> 
    html_element(".indicator-item__data-info-year") |> 
    html_text2() |> 
    str_extract("\\d{4}") |> 
    as.numeric()
    
    # Put page elements into a data frame / tibble
    tibble(life_exp = life_exp,
           year = year)}
# webscrape in batches
africa_life_exp <- map(.x = africa_country_urls$link, 
                       .f = scrape_country_page) |>
                   bind_rows()

amro_life_exp <- map(.x = amro_country_urls$link, 
                     .f = scrape_country_page) |>
                 bind_rows()

emro_life_exp <- map(.x = emro_country_urls$link, 
                     .f = scrape_country_page) |>
                 bind_rows()

euro_life_exp <- map(.x = euro_country_urls$link, 
                     .f = scrape_country_page) |>
                 bind_rows()

searo_life_exp <- map(.x = searo_country_urls$link, 
                     .f = scrape_country_page) |>
                 bind_rows()

wpro_life_exp <- map(.x = wpro_country_urls$link, 
                     .f = scrape_country_page) |>
                 bind_rows()


# combine countries and life expectancies
africa_life_exp <- cbind(africa_country_urls, africa_life_exp)
amro_life_exp <- cbind(amro_country_urls, amro_life_exp) 
emro_life_exp <- cbind(emro_country_urls, emro_life_exp)
euro_life_exp <- cbind(euro_country_urls, euro_life_exp)
searo_life_exp <- cbind(searo_country_urls, searo_life_exp) 
wpro_life_exp <- cbind(wpro_country_urls, wpro_life_exp) 
all_life_exp <- bind_rows(africa_life_exp, amro_life_exp, emro_life_exp,
                          euro_life_exp, searo_life_exp, wpro_life_exp)

# save to csv
all_life_exp |>
  write_csv("measles_life_expectancy_data.csv")
# load in csv 
life_exp_data <- read.csv(here::here("data", "measles_life_expectancy_data.csv"))
life_exp_data <- life_exp_data |>
  select(-c(link, year)) |>
  rename(Country = country, 
         `Life Expectancy 2024` = life_exp)

head(life_exp_data)
       Country Life Expectancy 2024
1      Algeria                   76
2       Angola                   65
3        Benin                   61
4     Botswana                   69
5 Burkina Faso                   61
6      Burundi                   64

Join and Clean DF

Join temperature and life expectancy to measles_year dataframe

# join temperature
measles_year <- measles_year |>
  left_join(temperature_df, by = "Country") |>
  left_join(life_exp_data, by = c("ASCII" = "Country"))

Summaries and Visualizations

# load world map data
library(rnaturalearthdata)
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

world_life_exp <- world |>
  left_join(measles_year |> 
            distinct(Country, `Life Expectancy 2024`), 
            by = c("name" = "Country"))

ggplot(data = world_life_exp) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = `Life Expectancy 2024`), 
          color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma", 
                       na.value = "grey80", name = "Years") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(title = "Global Life Expectancy at Birth (2024 Projection)",
       caption = "Countries within Africa tend to have an extremely lower lower life expectancy as opposed to other regions.",
       subtitle = "Source: https://data.worldbank.org/") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

plot_data <- measles_year |>
  group_by(Country, ASCII, Region, `Life Expectancy 2024`) |>
  summarize(total_rate_avg = mean(`Total Measles per Million`, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")

region_medians <- plot_data |>
  group_by(Region) |>
  summarize(median_life_exp = median(`Life Expectancy 2024`, na.rm = TRUE),
            median_rate = median(total_rate_avg, na.rm = TRUE))

region_medians |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = md(paste0("Median Measles Rate Across Each Region"))) |>
  cols_label("Region" = md("**Region**"),
               "median_life_exp" = md("**Median Life Expectancy (years)**"),
               "median_rate" = md("**Median Measles Rate**")) |>
  tab_source_note(md("Despite having the lowest median life expectancy, countries in Africa do not have the highest median Measles Rate."))
Median Measles Rate Across Each Region
Region Median Life Expectancy (years) Median Measles Rate
AFRO 64 25.37143
AMRO 75 0.01000
EMRO 75 26.05786
EURO 80 13.00000
SEARO 73 13.02538
WPRO 72 10.54143
Despite having the lowest median life expectancy, countries in Africa do not have the highest median Measles Rate. 
plot_data |>
ggplot(aes(x = `Life Expectancy 2024`, y = total_rate_avg, color = Region)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(data = region_medians, 
             aes(x = median_life_exp, y = median_rate), 
             shape = 18, 
             size = 4,
             color = "black") +
  geom_point(data = region_medians, 
             aes(x = median_life_exp, y = median_rate), 
             shape = 18, 
             size = 2) +     
  labs(title = "Average Measles Rate vs. Life Expectancy",
       subtitle = "Diamonds represent the regional medians",
       x = "Life Expectancy at Birth (years)",
       y = "")

API

REST API

Data Description: This is country-level data with 45 potential fields. We obtained the 9 most important variables for our analyses: country name, geolocation (longitude & latitude), population density, border nations, gini, hdi, government type, and religion. We plan on looking at measles rates related to socioeconomic variables like government, religion, hdi, and gini. We also plan on looking at measles rates related to geographic factors like border nations and geolocation. We need variables like cca3 to use in identifying country matches to join to our other dataframes.

Note: Variables like border are maintained in lists due to their varying lengths.

Source: https://restcountries.com/

world_df <- GET("https://restcountries.com/v4/all?fields=cca3,geolocation,area,borders,hdi,gini,government,religion") |> 
  (\(x) rawToChar(x$content))() |> 
  fromJSON() |>
  unnest_wider(c(gini, geolocation, government, religion), "_") |>
  relocate(cca3) |>
  rename("ISO3" = cca3) |>
  arrange(ISO3)

world_df |>
  head(5) |>
  select(-government_leaders) |>
  gt() |> tab_header(title = md("REST API Data")) |>
    cols_label(ISO3 = md("**ISO3**"),
               gini_year = md("**GINI Year**"),
               gini_value = md("**GINI Value**"),
               geolocation_latitude = md("**Latitude**"),
               geolocation_longitude = md("**Longitude**"),
               religion_name = md("**Religions**"),
               religion_population = md("**Religion Populations**"),
               religion_percentage = md("**Religion Percentages**"),
               government_type = md("**Government Type**"),
               #government_leaders = md("**Government Leaders**"),
               hdi = md("**Human Development Index**"),
               borders = md("**Border Nations**"),
               area = md("**Land Area**")) |>
  fmt_number(decimals = 3, use_seps = TRUE) |>
  tab_caption(md("Table 1. Variables left in lists have varying lengths"))
Table 1. Variables left in lists have varying lengths

AGO 2018 51.3 −12.500 18.500 christianity, no religion, other 29069176, 4226239, 3454491 79.1, 11.5, 9.4 Unitary presidential republic 0.616 COG, COD, ZMB, NAM 1,246,700.000

GDP API

Data Description: We also have another API dataset that contains GDP data for each country. We are interested in seeing how a country’s (or region’s) GDP impacts their measles rates. The variables include:

  • gdp_growth: GDP growth rate as a percentage
  • gdp_nominal: Nominal GDP in billions of US dollars
  • gdp_per_capita_nominal: Nominal GDP per capita in US dollars
  • gdp_ppp: GDP at purchasing power parity (PPP) in billions of US dollars
  • gdp_per_capita_ppp: GDP per capita at PPP in US dollars
  • gdp_ppp_share: Country’s share of world GDP at PPP as a percentage

Source: https://api-ninjas.com/api/gdp

Base URL for GDP Data API:

# base url for GDP API
base_url <- "https://api.api-ninjas.com/v1/gdp?country="

Function to get GDP info by country code:

gdp_info <- function(country_code) {
  url <- str_c(base_url, country_code, 
               #"&year=", as.character(year),
               "&X-Api-Key=lJsfvISfAdepHh5moYOXLLFjSKBr5MYFQ1E9V7YN")
  
  rawToChar(GET(url)$content) |>
    fromJSON()
}

Obtaining all unique country codes in measles_year and mapping to gdp_info function:

# Using map to iterate through unique country codes in measles_year
gdp_data <- map_dfr(
  unique(measles_year$`Country Code`),
  gdp_info
  ) |>
  filter(year > 2011) # Keeping years 2012 and on since that is what we have in our measles data
write.csv(gdp_data, "gdp_data.csv")

Join and Clean API Data

measles_year_clean <- measles_year |>
  left_join(world_df, by = c("Country Code" = "ISO3")) |>
  left_join(gdp_data,
            by = c("Country Code" = "country",
                   "Year" = "year")
            ) |>
  mutate(Density = `Total Pop.`/area) |>
  select(-c(ASCII, Continent, "Annualized Pop. 2025"))

Summaries & Visualizations

measles_year_clean |>
  head(5) |>
  gt() |> 
  tab_header(title = md("Combined Data Sets")) |>
    cols_label(Region = md("**Region**"),
               Country = md("**Country**"),
               "Country Code" = md("**Country Code**"),
               Year = md("**Year**"),
               "Total Pop." = md("**Total Pop.**"),
               Density = md("**Population Density (People/km^2)**"),
              "Total Suspected Cases" = md("**Total Suspected Cases**"),
              "Total Measles" = md("**Total Measles Cases**"),
               "Total Lab Measles" = md("**Total Lab Measles**"),
              "Total Epi Measles" = md("**Total Epidem Measles**"),
              "Total Clinical Measles" = md("**Clinical Measles Cases**"),
              "Total Measles per Million" = md("**Measles Rate Per Million**"),
              "Total Discarded" = md("**Discarded Cases**"),
              "Total Discarded per Million" = md("**Discarded Rate Per Million**"),
               gini_year = md("**GINI Year**"),
               gini_value = md("**GINI Value**"),
               geolocation_latitude = md("**Latitude**"),
               geolocation_longitude = md("**Longitude**"),
               religion_name = md("**Religions**"),
               religion_population = md("**Religion Populations**"),
               religion_percentage = md("**Religion Percentages**"),
               government_type = md("**Government Type**"),
               government_leaders = md("**Government Leaders**"),
               hdi = md("**Human Development Index**"),
               borders = md("**Border Nations**"),
               area = md("**Land Area**"),
               "Avg. Celsius" = md("**Avg. Celsius**"),
               "Avg. Fahrenheit" = md("**Avg. Fahrenheit**"),
               "Life Expectancy 2024" = md("**Life Expectancy 2024**"),
               gdp_growth = md("**GDP Growth (%)**"),
               gdp_nominal = md("**Nominal GDP (Billions USD)**"),
               gdp_per_capita_nominal = md("**Nominal GDP per Capita (USD)**"),
               gdp_ppp = md("**GDP (PPP, Billions USD)**"),
               gdp_per_capita_ppp = md("**GDP per Capita (PPP, USD)**"),
               gdp_ppp_share = md("**Share of World GDP (PPP, %)**")) |>
  fmt_number(decimals = 3,
             columns = -c(Year, geolocation_latitude, geolocation_longitude),
             use_seps = TRUE) |>
  tab_caption(md("Table 2. REST API Data joined with annual measles data"))

measles_year_clean |>
  group_by(Country) |>
  summarise(
    Avg_Pop = mean(`Total Pop.`, na.rm = TRUE),
    Avg_Density = mean(Density, na.rm = TRUE),
    Avg_Measles_Rate = mean(`Total Measles per Million`, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop") |>
  filter("Avg_Pop" >= 100000) |>
  slice_max(Avg_Density, n = 5) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = md("Most Population-Dense Countries (Historical Average)")) |>
  cols_label(
    Country = md("**Country**"),
    Avg_Pop = md("**Avg. Population**"),
    Avg_Density = md("**Avg. Density (People/km^2)**"),
    Avg_Measles_Rate = md("**Avg. Measles Rate Per Million**")) |>
  fmt_number(decimals = 3, use_seps = TRUE) |>
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()) |>
  tab_caption("Table 3. Values represent the mean across all years present in the dataset. Average Population is at least 100k.")
Table 3. Values represent the mean across all years present in the dataset. Average Population is at least 100k.
Most Population-Dense Countries (Historical Average)
Country Avg. Population Avg. Density (People/km^2) Avg. Measles Rate Per Million
Monaco 36,671.000 18,153.960 40.290

Bahrain 1,456,374.786 1,851.010 5.715
Maldives 472,638.143 1,575.460 3.946
Malta 470,409.000 1,488.636 9.893
 
measles_year_clean |>
  slice_max(`Total Measles per Million`, n = 5) |>
  select(Country, Year, Density, `Total Measles per Million`) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = md("Countries With Greatest Measles Rate")) |>
  fmt_number(decimals = 3, use_seps = TRUE,
             columns = -Year) |>
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()) |>
  tab_caption("Table 4. Countries with high measles rates appear to have relatively low population densities")
Table 4. Countries with high measles rates appear to have relatively low population densities

Country Year Density Total Measles per Million

Madagascar 2019 48.071 7,558.270

Kyrgyzstan 2015 30.017 2,962.240

CSV

In a .csv file already, so it could not be scraped, but I thought it would be interesting.

Measles Vaccine (1st Dose) Immunization

Data Description: The WHO has data on measles containing vaccine first-dose (MCV1) immunization coverage among one year olds.

Motivation: Measles is highly contagious, with children under five facing the greatest risk of severe health complications. As a result, examining global coverage rates for the first dose of the measles vaccine is especially important.

Source: https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/measles-containing-vaccine-first-dose-(mcv1)-immunization-coverage-among-1-year-olds-(-)

https://www.cdc.gov/measles/about/index.html

mcv <-read_csv(here::here("data", "mcv1.csv"))

mcv <- mcv |> 
  select(SpatialDimValueCode, Period, Value) |>
  rename("Country Code" = SpatialDimValueCode,
         "MCV1 Coverage" = Value,
         "MCV1 Year" = Period) 

# pivot to make clearer
measles_year_clean <- measles_year_clean |>
  left_join(mcv, by = join_by("Country Code", "Year" == "MCV1 Year"))

Visualization

measles_year_clean |>
  group_by(Region, Year) |>
  summarise(mean_coverage = mean(`MCV1 Coverage`, na.rm = TRUE), 
            .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Year, y = mean_coverage, color = Region)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Average MCV1 Coverage (%) for 1 Year Olds Across Time",
       subtitle = "Source: WHO",
       y = "",
       x = "Year",
       caption = "The average measles vaccine coverage is substantially lower for countries in Africa than other regions.
       We also see a dip in coverage globally around the start of the Covid pandemic.") +
  theme_minimal()

Join and Clean Final Dataset

# not showing warning message because some rows have more than 3 religions, but
# we are only keeping 3
measles_year_clean <- measles_year_clean |>
  rename(`Gini Year` = gini_year,
         `Gini Value` = gini_value,
         "Latitude" = geolocation_latitude,
         "Longitude" = geolocation_longitude,
         "Religion" = religion_name,
         "Religion Pop." = religion_population,
         "Pct Religious" = religion_percentage,
         "Govt. Type" = government_type,
         "HDI" = hdi,
         "Borders" = borders,
         "Area" = area,
         "Govt. Leaders" = government_leaders,
         `GDP Growth (%)` = gdp_growth,
         `Nominal GDP` = gdp_nominal,
         `Nominal GDP per Capita` = gdp_per_capita_nominal,
         `GDP PPP` = gdp_ppp,
         `GDP per capita PPP` = gdp_per_capita_ppp,
         `Share of World GDP PPP (%)` = gdp_ppp_share
         )

# clean gini data
gini_data <- measles_year_clean |>
  select(`Gini Year`, `Gini Value`, `Country Code`, "Year")

measles_year_clean <- measles_year_clean |>
  select(-c(`Gini Year`, `Gini Value`))
  
gini_data <- gini_data |>
  unnest(cols = c(`Gini Year`, `Gini Value`)) |>
  mutate(
    `Gini Year` = as.numeric(as.character(`Gini Year`)),
    `Gini Value` = as.numeric(as.character(`Gini Value`))) |>
  filter(Year == `Gini Year`) |>
  select(`Country Code`, Year, `Gini Value`)

measles_final <- measles_year_clean |>
  left_join(gini_data, by = c("Country Code", "Year"))

Final Dataset

measles_final <- measles_final |>
  # Converting list-columns to character strings to make it easier to parse
  mutate(
    Religion = map_chr(Religion, ~ paste(.x, collapse = ", ")),
    `Religion Pop.` = map_chr(`Religion Pop.`, ~ paste(.x, collapse = ", ")),
    `Pct Religious` = map_chr(`Pct Religious`, ~ paste(.x, collapse = ", "))
  ) |>
  # Cleaning spacing around commas
  mutate(
    Religion = str_replace_all(Religion, ",\\s*", ","),
    `Religion Pop.` = str_replace_all(`Religion Pop.`, ",\\s*", ","),
    `Pct Religious` = str_replace_all(`Pct Religious`, ",\\s*", ",")
  ) |>
  # Gathering top 3 religions for specific country and year
  separate(Religion,
           into = c("Primary Religion", "Secondary Religion",
                    "Tertiary Religion"),
           sep = ",", fill = "right") |>
  
  separate(`Religion Pop.`,
           into = c("Primary Religion Pop", "Secondary Religion Pop",
                    "Tertiary Religion Pop"),
           sep = ",", fill = "right") |>
  
  separate(`Pct Religious`,
           into = c("Primary Pct Religious", "Secondary Pct Religious",
                    "Tertiary Pct Religious"),
           sep = ",", fill = "right") |>
  # converting Pop and Pct to numeric variables
  mutate(
    across(contains("Pop"),
           ~ as.numeric(str_replace_all(.x, ",", ""))),
    across(contains("Pct"),
           as.numeric)
  )

  select(-`Govt. Leaders`) |>
  head()

#   `Tertiary Religion` <chr>, `Primary Religion Pop` <dbl>, …
# save to csv
measles_final |>
  write_csv("measles_final.csv")
Source Code
---
title: "project-1-checkpoint3"
author: "Cameron An"
format: html
embed-resources: true
editor: visual
code-tools: true
---

# External Data

## Libraries

```{r}
#| message: false
# libraries
library(rvest)
library(tidyverse)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(sf)
library(gt)
library(httr)
library(tidyjson)
library(jsonlite)
library(glue)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)

# measles_year dataset
measles_year <- read_csv(here::here("data", "measles_year.csv"))
head(measles_year)
```

## Motivation:

Measles continues to affect millions of people worldwide each year, despite major scientific advancements and the availability of an effective vaccine. We aim to explore the socioeconomic and geographic factors that may influence measles infection rates. By analyzing these patterns, we hope to better understand disparities across regions and identify populations at higher risk. Countries in Africa are of particular interest, as they are among the most heavily impacted by the measles disease.

**Source:** <https://www.who.int/news/item/05-12-2019-more-than-140-000-die-from-measles-as-cases-surge-worldwide>

# Webscraping:

Like many diseases, measles transmission is influenced by environmental conditions. Research suggests that the virus is less likely to thrive in extremely cold or hot temperatures, while lower relative humidity can make it easier to spread. It is also important to consider a country's life expectancy at birth, as it offers insight into overall healthcare quality and the level of development.

**Source:** <https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.4161/hv.27826#d1e168>

### get_info_from_page():

pulls information from a website.

```{r}
get_info_from_page <- function(page, css_selector, node, type) {
  
  # grab the css and node elements from the page
  if (type == "temp"){
  page |>
    html_element(css_selector) |>
    html_elements(node) |>
    html_text(trim = TRUE)}
  
  else if(type == "url"){
  page |>
    html_elements(node) |> 
    html_attr("href")}
  
  else {
  page |>
    html_elements(node) |> 
    html_text2()}}
```

## Scrape Average Yearly Temperature

**Source:** <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_countries_by_average_yearly_temperature>

**Data Description:** Wikipedia has data on the average yearly temperature across multiple countries.

### scrape_temp_page():

calls get_info_from_page() and grabs different elements, combining into clean dataframe.

```{r}
scrape_temp_page <- function(url) {
    
    # 1 second crawl delay
    Sys.sleep(1)
    
    # read in page
    page <- read_html(url)
    
    # country/region
    country_region <- get_info_from_page(page, 
                                         css_selector = "table.wikitable", 
                                         node = "tbody tr td:nth-child(2)",
                                         type = "temp")
      
    # continent
    continent <- get_info_from_page(page,
                                    css_selector = "table.wikitable", 
                                    node = "tbody tr td:nth-child(3)",
                                    type = "temp")
    
    # temp 
    temp <- get_info_from_page(page, 
                               css_selector = "table.wikitable", 
                               node = "tbody tr td:nth-child(4)",
                               type = "temp")
    
    # Put page elements into a data frame / tibble
    tibble(Country = country_region,
           Continent = continent,
           Temperature = temp)}

```

```{r}
# apply to temp page
url <- "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_countries_by_average_yearly_temperature"
temperature_df <- scrape_temp_page(url)
```

clean temperature dataframe:

```{r}
# split temperature col into celsius and fahrenheit
temperature_df <- temperature_df |>
  mutate(`Avg. Celsius` = as.numeric(str_extract(Temperature, "\\d+\\.\\d+")),
         `Avg. Fahrenheit` = as.numeric(str_extract(Temperature, "(?<=\\()\\d+\\.\\d+"))) |>
  select(-Temperature)

head(temperature_df)
```

## Scrape Life Expectancy

**Source:** <https://data.worldbank.org/country>

**Data Description:** The world bank group has large amounts of data relating to a given country's socioeconomic factors.

### grab_urls():

scrapes the data.worldbank.org page for all of the urls for each country

```{r}
grab_urls <- function(country_url){
  # read page
  page <- read_html(country_url)
    
  # links
  country_links <- get_info_from_page(page, 
                                      css_selector = "", 
                                      node = "section.nav-item ul li a",
                                      type = "url")
  
  # country
  country <- get_info_from_page(page, 
                                css_selector = "", 
                                node = "section.nav-item ul li a",
                                type = "text")
  # full url
  tibble(link = paste0("https://data.worldbank.org", country_links),
         country = country)}
```

Keep relevant countries for more efficient webscraping

```{r}
# grab urls
country_url <- "https://data.worldbank.org/country"
all_country_urls <- grab_urls(country_url)

# countries are not exactly named the same
measles_year |>
  filter(!ASCII %in% all_country_urls$country) |>
  pull(Country) |>
  unique()

# clean country col to match
all_country_urls <- all_country_urls |>
  separate_wider_delim(country, 
                       delim = ",", 
                       names = c("country", "country_extra"), 
                       too_few = "align_start") |>
  mutate(country = str_replace_all(country, "(?i)st\\.", "Saint"),
         country = case_when(str_detect(country_extra, "Dem\\.") ~ paste("Democratic", country),
                             TRUE ~ country),
         country = recode_values(country,
                                 "Lao PDR" ~ "Lao",
                                 "Kyrgyz Republic" ~ "Kyrgyzstan",
                                 "Slovak Republic" ~ "Slovakia",
                                 default = country)) |>
         select(-country_extra)


# clean ASCII col to match
measles_year <- measles_year |>
  mutate(ASCII = str_replace_all(ASCII, "\\s*\\(.*?\\)", ""),
         ASCII = str_remove_all(ASCII, "(?i)\\bRepublic of (the )?"),
         ASCII = str_remove_all(ASCII, "(?i)\\bPeople's\\s*"),
         ASCII = recode_values(ASCII,
                          "United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland" ~ "Ireland",
                          "United States of America" ~ "United States",
                          "Lao Democratic Republic" ~ "Lao",
                          "United Tanzania" ~ "Tanzania",
                          "Democratic Republic of the Congo" ~ "Democratic Congo",
                          default = ASCII),
         ASCII = str_trim(ASCII))

# check changes were made
measles_year |>
  filter(!ASCII %in% all_country_urls$country) |>
  pull(Country) |>
  unique()
```

The website does not like large calls, so we have to resort to batches.

```{r}
# grab africa countries 
measles_africa <- measles_year |>
  filter(Region == "AFRO")

# keep only countries in measles_year 
africa_country_urls <- all_country_urls |>
  filter(country %in% measles_africa$ASCII)

# grab amro countries
measles_amro <- measles_year |>
  filter(Region == "AMRO")

amro_country_urls <- all_country_urls |>
  filter(country %in% measles_amro$ASCII)

# grab emro countries
measles_emro <- measles_year |>
  filter(Region == "EMRO")

emro_country_urls <- all_country_urls |>
  filter(country %in% measles_emro$ASCII)

# grab euro countries
measles_euro <- measles_year |>
  filter(Region == "EURO")

euro_country_urls <- all_country_urls |>
  filter(country %in% measles_euro$ASCII)

# grab searo countries
measles_searo <- measles_year |>
  filter(Region == "SEARO")

searo_country_urls <- all_country_urls |>
  filter(country %in% measles_searo$ASCII)

# grab wpro countries
measles_wpro <- measles_year |>
  filter(Region == "WPRO")

wpro_country_urls <- all_country_urls |>
  filter(country %in% measles_wpro$ASCII)
```

### scrape_country_page():

scrapes a country's data.worldbank.org page for its life expectancy

```{r}
scrape_country_page <- function(url) {
    
    # 1 second crawl delay
    Sys.sleep(runif(1, min = 4, max = 6))
    
    # read in page
    page <- read_html(url)
    
    # le_node
    le_node <- page |> 
    html_elements(".indicator-item") |> 
    keep(~str_detect(html_text(.x), "Life expectancy at birth"))
      
    # life_exp
    life_exp <- le_node |> 
    html_element(".indicator-item__data-info span") |> 
    html_text2() |> 
    as.numeric()
    
    # year 
    year <- le_node |> 
    html_element(".indicator-item__data-info-year") |> 
    html_text2() |> 
    str_extract("\\d{4}") |> 
    as.numeric()
    
    # Put page elements into a data frame / tibble
    tibble(life_exp = life_exp,
           year = year)}
```

```{r}
#| cache: true
#| eval: false
# webscrape in batches
africa_life_exp <- map(.x = africa_country_urls$link, 
                       .f = scrape_country_page) |>
                   bind_rows()

amro_life_exp <- map(.x = amro_country_urls$link, 
                     .f = scrape_country_page) |>
                 bind_rows()

emro_life_exp <- map(.x = emro_country_urls$link, 
                     .f = scrape_country_page) |>
                 bind_rows()

euro_life_exp <- map(.x = euro_country_urls$link, 
                     .f = scrape_country_page) |>
                 bind_rows()

searo_life_exp <- map(.x = searo_country_urls$link, 
                     .f = scrape_country_page) |>
                 bind_rows()

wpro_life_exp <- map(.x = wpro_country_urls$link, 
                     .f = scrape_country_page) |>
                 bind_rows()


# combine countries and life expectancies
africa_life_exp <- cbind(africa_country_urls, africa_life_exp)
amro_life_exp <- cbind(amro_country_urls, amro_life_exp) 
emro_life_exp <- cbind(emro_country_urls, emro_life_exp)
euro_life_exp <- cbind(euro_country_urls, euro_life_exp)
searo_life_exp <- cbind(searo_country_urls, searo_life_exp) 
wpro_life_exp <- cbind(wpro_country_urls, wpro_life_exp) 
all_life_exp <- bind_rows(africa_life_exp, amro_life_exp, emro_life_exp,
                          euro_life_exp, searo_life_exp, wpro_life_exp)

# save to csv
all_life_exp |>
  write_csv("measles_life_expectancy_data.csv")
```

```{r}
# load in csv 
life_exp_data <- read.csv(here::here("data", "measles_life_expectancy_data.csv"))
life_exp_data <- life_exp_data |>
  select(-c(link, year)) |>
  rename(Country = country, 
         `Life Expectancy 2024` = life_exp)

head(life_exp_data)
```

## Join and Clean DF

Join temperature and life expectancy to measles_year dataframe

```{r}
# join temperature
measles_year <- measles_year |>
  left_join(temperature_df, by = "Country") |>
  left_join(life_exp_data, by = c("ASCII" = "Country"))

head(measles_year)
```

## Summaries and Visualizations

```{r}
#| warning: false
# load world map data
library(rnaturalearthdata)
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

world_life_exp <- world |>
  left_join(measles_year |> 
            distinct(Country, `Life Expectancy 2024`), 
            by = c("name" = "Country"))

ggplot(data = world_life_exp) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = `Life Expectancy 2024`), 
          color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma", 
                       na.value = "grey80", name = "Years") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(title = "Global Life Expectancy at Birth (2024 Projection)",
       caption = "Countries within Africa tend to have an extremely lower lower life expectancy as opposed to other regions.",
       subtitle = "Source: https://data.worldbank.org/") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r}
#| warning: false
plot_data <- measles_year |>
  group_by(Country, ASCII, Region, `Life Expectancy 2024`) |>
  summarize(total_rate_avg = mean(`Total Measles per Million`, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")

region_medians <- plot_data |>
  group_by(Region) |>
  summarize(median_life_exp = median(`Life Expectancy 2024`, na.rm = TRUE),
            median_rate = median(total_rate_avg, na.rm = TRUE))

region_medians |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = md(paste0("Median Measles Rate Across Each Region"))) |>
  cols_label("Region" = md("**Region**"),
               "median_life_exp" = md("**Median Life Expectancy (years)**"),
               "median_rate" = md("**Median Measles Rate**")) |>
  tab_source_note(md("Despite having the lowest median life expectancy, countries in Africa do not have the highest median Measles Rate."))
```

```{r}
#| warning: false
plot_data |>
ggplot(aes(x = `Life Expectancy 2024`, y = total_rate_avg, color = Region)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(data = region_medians, 
             aes(x = median_life_exp, y = median_rate), 
             shape = 18, 
             size = 4,
             color = "black") +
  geom_point(data = region_medians, 
             aes(x = median_life_exp, y = median_rate), 
             shape = 18, 
             size = 2) +     
  labs(title = "Average Measles Rate vs. Life Expectancy",
       subtitle = "Diamonds represent the regional medians",
       x = "Life Expectancy at Birth (years)",
       y = "")
```

# API

## REST API

**Data Description:** This is country-level data with 45 potential fields. We obtained the 9 most important variables for our analyses: country name, geolocation (longitude & latitude), population density, border nations, gini, hdi, government type, and religion. We plan on looking at measles rates related to socioeconomic variables like government, religion, hdi, and gini. We also plan on looking at measles rates related to geographic factors like border nations and geolocation. We need variables like cca3 to use in identifying country matches to join to our other dataframes. 

**Note**: Variables like border are maintained in lists due to their varying lengths.

**Source:** [https://restcountries.com/ ](https://restcountries.com/)

```{r}
world_df <- GET("https://restcountries.com/v4/all?fields=cca3,geolocation,area,borders,hdi,gini,government,religion") |> 
  (\(x) rawToChar(x$content))() |> 
  fromJSON() |>
  unnest_wider(c(gini, geolocation, government, religion), "_") |>
  relocate(cca3) |>
  rename("ISO3" = cca3) |>
  arrange(ISO3)

world_df |>
  head(5) |>
  select(-government_leaders) |>
  gt() |> tab_header(title = md("REST API Data")) |>
    cols_label(ISO3 = md("**ISO3**"),
               gini_year = md("**GINI Year**"),
               gini_value = md("**GINI Value**"),
               geolocation_latitude = md("**Latitude**"),
               geolocation_longitude = md("**Longitude**"),
               religion_name = md("**Religions**"),
               religion_population = md("**Religion Populations**"),
               religion_percentage = md("**Religion Percentages**"),
               government_type = md("**Government Type**"),
               #government_leaders = md("**Government Leaders**"),
               hdi = md("**Human Development Index**"),
               borders = md("**Border Nations**"),
               area = md("**Land Area**")) |>
  fmt_number(decimals = 3, use_seps = TRUE) |>
  tab_caption(md("Table 1. Variables left in lists have varying lengths"))

```


## GDP API

**Data Description:** We also have another API dataset that contains GDP data for each country. We are interested in seeing how a country's (or region's) GDP impacts their measles rates. The variables include:

* *gdp_growth*: GDP growth rate as a percentage
* *gdp_nominal*: Nominal GDP in billions of US dollars
* *gdp_per_capita_nominal*: Nominal GDP per capita in US dollars
* *gdp_ppp*: GDP at purchasing power parity (PPP) in billions of US dollars
* *gdp_per_capita_ppp*: GDP per capita at PPP in US dollars
* *gdp_ppp_share*: Country's share of world GDP at PPP as a percentage

**Source:** [https://api-ninjas.com/api/gdp](https://api-ninjas.com/api/gdp)

Base URL for GDP Data API:

```{r}
# base url for GDP API
base_url <- "https://api.api-ninjas.com/v1/gdp?country="
```

Function to get GDP info by country code:

```{r}
gdp_info <- function(country_code) {
  url <- str_c(base_url, country_code, 
               #"&year=", as.character(year),
               "&X-Api-Key=lJsfvISfAdepHh5moYOXLLFjSKBr5MYFQ1E9V7YN")
  
  rawToChar(GET(url)$content) |>
    fromJSON()
}

```

Obtaining all unique country codes in measles_year and mapping to gdp_info function:

```{r}
# Using map to iterate through unique country codes in measles_year
gdp_data <- map_dfr(
  unique(measles_year$`Country Code`),
  gdp_info
  ) |>
  filter(year > 2011) # Keeping years 2012 and on since that is what we have in our measles data
```

```{r}
write.csv(gdp_data, "gdp_data.csv")
```

## Join and Clean API Data

```{r}
measles_year_clean <- measles_year |>
  left_join(world_df, by = c("Country Code" = "ISO3")) |>
  left_join(gdp_data,
            by = c("Country Code" = "country",
                   "Year" = "year")
            ) |>
  mutate(Density = `Total Pop.`/area) |>
  select(-c(ASCII, Continent, "Annualized Pop. 2025"))
  
```

## Summaries & Visualizations

```{r}
measles_year_clean |>
  head(5) |>
  gt() |> 
  tab_header(title = md("Combined Data Sets")) |>
    cols_label(Region = md("**Region**"),
               Country = md("**Country**"),
               "Country Code" = md("**Country Code**"),
               Year = md("**Year**"),
               "Total Pop." = md("**Total Pop.**"),
               Density = md("**Population Density (People/km^2)**"),
              "Total Suspected Cases" = md("**Total Suspected Cases**"),
              "Total Measles" = md("**Total Measles Cases**"),
               "Total Lab Measles" = md("**Total Lab Measles**"),
              "Total Epi Measles" = md("**Total Epidem Measles**"),
              "Total Clinical Measles" = md("**Clinical Measles Cases**"),
              "Total Measles per Million" = md("**Measles Rate Per Million**"),
              "Total Discarded" = md("**Discarded Cases**"),
              "Total Discarded per Million" = md("**Discarded Rate Per Million**"),
               gini_year = md("**GINI Year**"),
               gini_value = md("**GINI Value**"),
               geolocation_latitude = md("**Latitude**"),
               geolocation_longitude = md("**Longitude**"),
               religion_name = md("**Religions**"),
               religion_population = md("**Religion Populations**"),
               religion_percentage = md("**Religion Percentages**"),
               government_type = md("**Government Type**"),
               government_leaders = md("**Government Leaders**"),
               hdi = md("**Human Development Index**"),
               borders = md("**Border Nations**"),
               area = md("**Land Area**"),
               "Avg. Celsius" = md("**Avg. Celsius**"),
               "Avg. Fahrenheit" = md("**Avg. Fahrenheit**"),
               "Life Expectancy 2024" = md("**Life Expectancy 2024**"),
               gdp_growth = md("**GDP Growth (%)**"),
               gdp_nominal = md("**Nominal GDP (Billions USD)**"),
               gdp_per_capita_nominal = md("**Nominal GDP per Capita (USD)**"),
               gdp_ppp = md("**GDP (PPP, Billions USD)**"),
               gdp_per_capita_ppp = md("**GDP per Capita (PPP, USD)**"),
               gdp_ppp_share = md("**Share of World GDP (PPP, %)**")) |>
  fmt_number(decimals = 3,
             columns = -c(Year, geolocation_latitude, geolocation_longitude),
             use_seps = TRUE) |>
  tab_caption(md("Table 2. REST API Data joined with annual measles data"))
```

```{r}
measles_year_clean |>
  group_by(Country) |>
  summarise(
    Avg_Pop = mean(`Total Pop.`, na.rm = TRUE),
    Avg_Density = mean(Density, na.rm = TRUE),
    Avg_Measles_Rate = mean(`Total Measles per Million`, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop") |>
  filter("Avg_Pop" >= 100000) |>
  slice_max(Avg_Density, n = 5) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = md("Most Population-Dense Countries (Historical Average)")) |>
  cols_label(
    Country = md("**Country**"),
    Avg_Pop = md("**Avg. Population**"),
    Avg_Density = md("**Avg. Density (People/km^2)**"),
    Avg_Measles_Rate = md("**Avg. Measles Rate Per Million**")) |>
  fmt_number(decimals = 3, use_seps = TRUE) |>
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()) |>
  tab_caption("Table 3. Values represent the mean across all years present in the dataset. Average Population is at least 100k.")
```

```{r}
measles_year_clean |>
  slice_max(`Total Measles per Million`, n = 5) |>
  select(Country, Year, Density, `Total Measles per Million`) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = md("Countries With Greatest Measles Rate")) |>
  fmt_number(decimals = 3, use_seps = TRUE,
             columns = -Year) |>
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()) |>
  tab_caption("Table 4. Countries with high measles rates appear to have relatively low population densities")
```

# CSV

In a .csv file already, so it could not be scraped, but I thought it would be interesting.

## Measles Vaccine (1st Dose) Immunization

**Data Description:** The WHO has data on measles containing vaccine first-dose (MCV1) immunization coverage among one year olds.

**Motivation:** Measles is highly contagious, with children under five facing the greatest risk of severe health complications. As a result, examining global coverage rates for the first dose of the measles vaccine is especially important.

**Source:** <https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/measles-containing-vaccine-first-dose-(mcv1)-immunization-coverage-among-1-year-olds-(-)>

<https://www.cdc.gov/measles/about/index.html>

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
mcv <-read_csv(here::here("data", "mcv1.csv"))

mcv <- mcv |> 
  select(SpatialDimValueCode, Period, Value) |>
  rename("Country Code" = SpatialDimValueCode,
         "MCV1 Coverage" = Value,
         "MCV1 Year" = Period) 

# pivot to make clearer
measles_year_clean <- measles_year_clean |>
  left_join(mcv, by = join_by("Country Code", "Year" == "MCV1 Year"))
```

## Visualization

```{r}
#| warning: false
measles_year_clean |>
  group_by(Region, Year) |>
  summarise(mean_coverage = mean(`MCV1 Coverage`, na.rm = TRUE), 
            .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Year, y = mean_coverage, color = Region)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Average MCV1 Coverage (%) for 1 Year Olds Across Time",
       subtitle = "Source: WHO",
       y = "",
       x = "Year",
       caption = "The average measles vaccine coverage is substantially lower for countries in Africa than other regions.
       We also see a dip in coverage globally around the start of the Covid pandemic.") +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Join and Clean Final Dataset

```{r, warning=FALSE}
# not showing warning message because some rows have more than 3 religions, but
# we are only keeping 3
measles_year_clean <- measles_year_clean |>
  rename(`Gini Year` = gini_year,
         `Gini Value` = gini_value,
         "Latitude" = geolocation_latitude,
         "Longitude" = geolocation_longitude,
         "Religion" = religion_name,
         "Religion Pop." = religion_population,
         "Pct Religious" = religion_percentage,
         "Govt. Type" = government_type,
         "HDI" = hdi,
         "Borders" = borders,
         "Area" = area,
         "Govt. Leaders" = government_leaders,
         `GDP Growth (%)` = gdp_growth,
         `Nominal GDP` = gdp_nominal,
         `Nominal GDP per Capita` = gdp_per_capita_nominal,
         `GDP PPP` = gdp_ppp,
         `GDP per capita PPP` = gdp_per_capita_ppp,
         `Share of World GDP PPP (%)` = gdp_ppp_share
         )

# clean gini data
gini_data <- measles_year_clean |>
  select(`Gini Year`, `Gini Value`, `Country Code`, "Year")

measles_year_clean <- measles_year_clean |>
  select(-c(`Gini Year`, `Gini Value`))
  
gini_data <- gini_data |>
  unnest(cols = c(`Gini Year`, `Gini Value`)) |>
  mutate(
    `Gini Year` = as.numeric(as.character(`Gini Year`)),
    `Gini Value` = as.numeric(as.character(`Gini Value`))) |>
  filter(Year == `Gini Year`) |>
  select(`Country Code`, Year, `Gini Value`)

measles_final <- measles_year_clean |>
  left_join(gini_data, by = c("Country Code", "Year"))
```

## Final Dataset

```{r}
measles_final <- measles_final |>
  # Converting list-columns to character strings to make it easier to parse
  mutate(
    Religion = map_chr(Religion, ~ paste(.x, collapse = ", ")),
    `Religion Pop.` = map_chr(`Religion Pop.`, ~ paste(.x, collapse = ", ")),
    `Pct Religious` = map_chr(`Pct Religious`, ~ paste(.x, collapse = ", "))
  ) |>
  # Cleaning spacing around commas
  mutate(
    Religion = str_replace_all(Religion, ",\\s*", ","),
    `Religion Pop.` = str_replace_all(`Religion Pop.`, ",\\s*", ","),
    `Pct Religious` = str_replace_all(`Pct Religious`, ",\\s*", ",")
  ) |>
  # Gathering top 3 religions for specific country and year
  separate(Religion,
           into = c("Primary Religion", "Secondary Religion",
                    "Tertiary Religion"),
           sep = ",", fill = "right") |>
  
  separate(`Religion Pop.`,
           into = c("Primary Religion Pop", "Secondary Religion Pop",
                    "Tertiary Religion Pop"),
           sep = ",", fill = "right") |>
  
  separate(`Pct Religious`,
           into = c("Primary Pct Religious", "Secondary Pct Religious",
                    "Tertiary Pct Religious"),
           sep = ",", fill = "right") |>
  # converting Pop and Pct to numeric variables
  mutate(
    across(contains("Pop"),
           ~ as.numeric(str_replace_all(.x, ",", ""))),
    across(contains("Pct"),
           as.numeric)
  )
```


```{r}
measles_final |>
  select(-`Govt. Leaders`) |>
  head()

# save to csv
measles_final |>
  write_csv("measles_final.csv")
```
